labels.name: accept_application
labels.description: Choose a human executor from pending applications. They will start working on the task.

labels.name: review_result
labels.description: Review a submitted result. action=approve pays the executor instantly (irreversible). action=revision returns it for rework with your comment. action=reject declines it and frees the slot. Always check result_text and result_file_url via get_task first.

labels.name: cancel_task
labels.description: Cancel a task. Refunds escrow for unfilled slots back to your balance. Fails if executors are currently working — review their results first.

labels.name: set_webhook
labels.description: Set an HTTPS URL to receive POST events: application.created, result.submitted, deposit.credited. Empty string disables.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: review_result, set_webhook · Private data: get_task · Public sink: set_webhook.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: review_result, set_webhook · Private data: get_task · Public sink: set_webhook

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
